config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa oleh mayoritas atau negara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan keadilan bagi tiap orang. Di Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu mudah bagi alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan perlakuan yang tidak adil terhadap individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuse by the majority or the state. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in practice; public safety, public health, and basic laws sometimes require limits, but those limits have to be narrow, justified, and accountable. If "society" becomes a vague excuse, it can swallow free speech, due process, and minority rights. To me, a healthy society exists to protect individuals, not to treat them as disposable for some abstract collective good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, padahal yang semestinya dilindungi justru warga dari penyalahgunaan kuasa mayoritas atau negara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi batasnya harus jelas: boleh membatasi hak hanya jika benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak melanggar martabat manusia. Kalau tidak, kata “masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi tameng untuk ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that logic has often been used to justify overreach by governments or majorities. That said, I also do not believe individual rights exist in a vacuum: public safety, disease control, and fair laws sometimes require limited restrictions, but those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable. The key point is that "society" is too vague and too easy to abuse if it is treated as a trump card over basic freedoms. A free society should protect people first, not turn them into sacrifices for an abstract collective good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, sementara yang punya kuasa justru lolos dari pengawasan. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya kalau benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, proporsional, dan tidak merendahkan martabat orang. Kalau tidak, “masyarakat” cuma jadi kata yang terdengar mulia untuk menutupi ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that kind of language has too often been used to excuse overreach by governments and majorities. I also do not buy the idea that “society” is some clear moral entity that can be trusted to outweigh basic freedoms; in practice, it often means powerful people deciding whose rights matter less. At the same time, I am not saying individual rights are unlimited, because public safety and public health can justify narrow restrictions, but only when they are clearly necessary, proportionate, and accountable. To me, a society is judged by how well it protects individuals, especially unpopular ones, not by how easily it can sacrifice them for a vague collective good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
